meta.title: Why Johnny Can’t Patch  Can Vulnerability Patching Work in the ICS World
meta.description:
Here one of the pioneers in SCADA security, Eric Byres of Tofino Security, presents "Why Johnny Can’t Patch: Can Vulnerability Patching Work in the ICS World?"

Eric looks at three examples and dives into questions such as:

- How many patches would be required on a sample system

- How many undetected/unannounced vulnerabilities are latent on the system

- The percentage of systems that incorrect and have an impact on the system including crashes or hangs

- Reboot issues and cost of personnel and patching (the oil rig / personnel safety impact was interesting)

- Reliance on backdoors and other vulnerabilities are relied on for 

- Patch acceptance by customers (data point, less than 10% are downloading security patches)

One of the examples is a no charge security patch for Tofino, where most customers would not take a free patch even after repeated contacts and urging by the vendor.
